caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Supplied role mix is 4-digit 5413 and does not represent the core survey occupations cleanly.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fractions are task judgments, not observed automation rates.
inputs.pi_dist: No US 541370 pricing-mix survey was located; this is not an observed billing mix.
inputs.pi_moat: Louisiana is an illustrative state rule, not a national market-share measure.
inputs.t50_years: No defined US 541370 AI-adoption survey was located; this is not a measured diffusion curve.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: BLS confirms technology use but does not measure generative-AI firm adoption.
inputs.historical_analogs: No dated, industry-specific adoption series was located.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: NSPS reports professional-surveyor age, not firm-owner age or an age distribution.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Workforce shortage is a proxy for, not a count of, sale-ready successors.
inputs.active_consolidators: This deliberately understates buyers not independently reopened.
inputs.buy_mult: Neither source provides target EBITDA; this is not a transaction-derived surveying multiple.
inputs.buy_mult: Owner compensation/SDE normalization is required in diligence.
inputs.exit_mult: Not supported by a disclosed surveying transaction multiple; no public-comparable bridge was calculated.
inputs.pricing_structure: Do not infer a percentage pricing mix from this selection.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $161,963 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541370); class <5: 4,125 firms, avg revenue/firm $264,017 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $41,319 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 1,456 firms, avg revenue/firm $810,381 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $126,825 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 758 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,761,821 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $275,725 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 469 firms, avg revenue/firm $5,603,267 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $876,911 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 76 firms, avg revenue/firm $16,153,224 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,527,980 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 43 firms, avg revenue/firm $32,923,953 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,152,599 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 119

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Field measurements and accountable boundary judgment create a floor, while office processing and generic coordination are exposed.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541370 ($4,275,045,767) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541370 ($8,875,752,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics and exact artifact bindings pass. All five registered sources were audited and support the packet's directional thesis, while the source record does not directly quantify several adoption, ownership, pricing, and multiple estimates. Those limitations are candidly disclosed in 14 authored caveats; no separate validator-authored caveat or score-changing material defect was found.

sources.title: BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook: Surveying and Mapping Technicians
sources.what_it_supports: Mixed field and office duties, use of drones/CAD/data processing, and BLS’s statement that technicians will still review and interpret technology output.

sources.title: Louisiana Administrative Code: standards for boundary surveys
sources.what_it_supports: Example state rule: boundary surveys require a licensed professional land surveyor or work under responsible charge and retain professional judgment.

sources.title: NSPS 2016 Hill Day Issues
sources.what_it_supports: NSPS states that the average professional surveyor age was 58 and retirements outpaced entrants; used only for documented workforce/succession shortage, not owner-age quantification.

sources.title: Bowman Acquires Nevada-based Smith & Associates Land Surveying
sources.what_it_supports: Direct evidence of a 2026 strategic acquisition of a land-surveying firm and reported $2.0m annualized net service billing.

sources.title: Bowman Consulting Group annual report acquisition note
sources.what_it_supports: 2021 acquisition note identifies 1519 Surveying, LLC as surveying/civil engineering and reports approximately $10.0m consideration; it does not report target EBITDA.

title: Surveying and Mapping (except Geophysical) Services
